content:
Why the future belongs to engineers who can define, design, verify, and govern — not merely generate code

16 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Created by Author using AIFor most of software history, writing code was expensive.
A developer had to understand the requirement, remember the syntax, study the framework, design the logic, handle edge cases, write tests, debug failures, and then explain the result to another human being.
Artificial intelligence is reducing the cost of several of those activities.
It can generate a class, draft a test suite, explain an unfamiliar codebase, propose a database schema, create a deployment script, review a pull request, summarize a production incident, and suggest a possible fix.
This is already changing the daily work of software developers. According to Stack Overflow’s 2025 Developer Survey, 84% of developers were using or planning to use AI tools. Yet only 29% said they trusted the output, a significant decline from the previous year.
That combination tells us almost everything we need to know:
AI adoption is becoming unavoidable, but confidence in unsupervised AI remains low.
